message.content:
Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-1, fixes applied vs main). Venv: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
The file /tmp/pass1_confirm.json is a JSON array of 22 pass-1 findings (each: issue, title, file, sev, problem, suggested_fix).
Constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign, no metered API, engine imports no adapter, lazy optional backends.

You are independent CONFIRMER #2 of 2 for finding INDEX 10 (0-based) in /tmp/pass1_confirm.json.
Read that item (its issue number, problem, suggested_fix, file). Then VERIFY THE APPLIED FIX on the current branch: read the changed source AND its regression test (git diff vs main, or just read the file + tests), and where useful run a PoC against the venv to confirm the original problem is actually resolved.
Return: appropriate = the fix correctly addresses the root cause in a sound way (not a hack/incomplete patch/over-claim); complete = the fix fully resolves the finding AND has a regression test that would catch a regression. Set either false if not, and say exactly what is missing. Echo the issue number in 'issue'.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify the applied fix for finding INDEX 10. Let me start by reading the finding.

input.command: cat /tmp/pass1_confirm.json | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(json.dumps(d[10], indent=2))"

content.content:
{
  "issue": "111",
  "title": "Graph-orchestration gate decisions never enter the hash-chained provenance trail \u2014 the documented \u201cappend-only trail\u201d for the durable path is unchained, untamper-evident LangGraph state",
  "file": "/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py",
  "sev": "MEDIUM",
  "problem": "There are two governance mechanisms (ADR-0010): the synchronous run_study path records every gate decision into the hash-chained ProvenanceTrail (provenance.py), and the durable LangGraph interrupt/resume path (gatenode.make_gate_node) records decisions as plain dicts appended to a LangGraph state list 'gate_decisions'. GOVERNANCE \u00a73 states the chained ProvenanceTrail \"is the spine\u2026 No step can be retroactively removed or reordered,\" and GOVERNANCE.md lines 47-50 call the graph's gate_decisions list \"the append-only trail.\" But the graph path's decisions are NEVER written into the chained Prov",
  "suggested_fix": "Bridge graph gate decisions into the chained trail: have make_gate_node (or a thin wrapper) accept a ProvenanceTrail-backed recorder and record each validated decision via trail.record('gate', ...) at apply time, so the durable path's approvals/rejections land in the same hash-chained, verify_records-checkable trail as the synchronous path. At minimum, reconcile the docs to stop calling the unchai"
}

1	"""Human-in-the-loop gate node — the interrupt/resume protocol (GOVERNANCE.md).
2	
3	:func:`make_gate_node` turns an engine :class:`~assay_engine.orchestration.gates.Gate` plus a
4	proposal builder into a LangGraph node that pauses the run at the gate (``interrupt``),
5	surfaces a proposal to the operator, and on resume validates and records the decision.
6	
7	The **gate-correlation guard** rejects a resumed decision aimed at a *different* gate than the
8	one currently parked — a stale or misrouted decision (e.g. delivered late through the
9	governance channel) must not be applied to whatever gate happens to be waiting. The guard's
10	expected gate is the gate the operator named in the resume value, compared against this node's
11	gate; auto-deriving it from the parked gate would make the guard always match and thus a no-op.
12	
13	Two further hardened behaviors, ported faithfully from the prior platform's gate node:
14	
15	- **Re-prompt on a bad resume (not raise).** ``interrupt()`` consumes and *persists* the
16	  resume value into the checkpoint *before* the node returns, so raising on a malformed or
17	  mis-correlated value would strand it in the checkpoint and replay it forever — the gate
18	  would be permanently bricked. Instead the node validates inside a loop and re-fires
19	  ``interrupt()`` (carrying the error) on a bad value, so the operator can re-submit and
20	  actually recover.
21	- **Terminal-decision idempotency.** An ``approved`` decision is terminal: re-entering an
22	  already-approved gate is a no-op (it does not re-prompt or re-record). ``rejected`` and
23	  ``deferred`` are deliberately NOT terminal, so a reject→revise→re-review loop re-fires the
24	  interrupt as intended.
25	
26	The node returns a partial state update appending one record to ``gate_decisions``; a study's
27	graph state should declare ``gate_decisions`` with a list/add reducer so records accumulate
28	across gates. The decision/recording validator (:func:`apply_gate_decision`) is pure and
29	tested offline; only the ``interrupt`` call requires LangGraph (the optional ``orchestration``
30	extra, imported lazily).
31	"""
32	
33	from __future__ import annotations
34	
35	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping
36	
37	from assay_engine.orchestration.gates import Gate, GateError
38	
39	GateProposal = Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], Mapping[str, Any]]
40	
41	_VALID_DECISIONS = frozenset({"approved", "rejected", "deferred"})
42	
43	
44	def apply_gate_decision(gate: Gate, resumed: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
45	    """Validate a resumed operator decision and return the state update to record it.
46	
47	    Raises :class:`GateError` if the resume value is malformed, targets a different gate
48	    (correlation guard), or carries an unrecognized decision.
49	    """
50	    if not isinstance(resumed, Mapping):
51	        raise GateError(
52	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: resume value must be a mapping, got {type(resumed).__name__}"
53	        )
54	    target = resumed.get("gate_id")
55	    if target != gate.name:
56	        raise GateError(
57	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: resumed decision targets {target!r} — stale or misrouted; "
58	            "rejecting (gate-correlation guard)"
59	        )
60	    decision = str(resumed.get("decision", "")).strip().lower()
61	    if decision not in _VALID_DECISIONS:
62	        raise GateError(
63	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: invalid decision {decision!r} "
64	            f"(expected one of {sorted(_VALID_DECISIONS)})"
65	        )
66	    # A rejection/deferral with no rationale is governance-relevant; record a sentinel rather
67	    # than a blank (incl. whitespace-only) so the audit trail is never silently empty.
68	    rationale = str(resumed.get("rationale") or "").strip() or "No rationale provided."
69	    record = {"gate": gate.name, "decision": decision, "rationale": rationale}
70	    return {"gate_decisions": [record]}
71	
72	
73	# Only an approval is terminal: a re-entered approved gate is a no-op, but a rejected/deferred
74	# gate must re-fire its interrupt so a revise→re-review loop can re-prompt the operator.
75	_TERMINAL_DECISIONS = frozenset({"approved"})
76	
77	
78	def already_decided(state: Mapping[str, Any], gate_name: str) -> bool:
79	    """True if ``gate_name`` already holds a terminal (approved) decision in ``gate_decisions``."""
80	    for record in state.get("gate_decisions", []) or []:
81	        if record.get("gate") == gate_name and record.get("decision") in _TERMINAL_DECISIONS:
82	            return True
83	    return False
84	
85	
86	GateRecorder = Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], None]
87	
88	
89	def make_gate_node(
90	    gate: Gate,
91	    propose: GateProposal,
92	    *,
93	    recorder: GateRecorder | None = None,
94	) -> Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], dict[str, Any]]:
95	    """Build a LangGraph node that parks at ``gate`` for an operator decision.
96	
97	    ``propose(state)`` returns the proposal payload surfaced to the operator (what the gate is
98	    asking them to approve). The node returns a ``{"gate_decisions": [record]}`` update, or
99	    ``{}`` (no-op) if the gate is already terminally approved.
100	
101	    ``recorder`` (optional) bridges each validated decision into the hash-chained provenance
102	    trail (#111): the durable LangGraph path otherwise records gate decisions only as
103	    (untamper-evident) graph state, so the documented append-only trail would miss them. Pass a
104	    callback such as ``lambda r: trail.record("gate", f"gate {r['gate']}: {r['decision']}", **r)``
105	    so the graph path's approvals/rejections land in the same chained trail as the runner's.
106	    """
107	
108	    def node(state: Mapping[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
109	        if already_decided(state, gate.name):
110	            return {}  # terminal decision exists — do not re-prompt or re-record
111	        try:
112	            from langgraph.types import interrupt
113	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
114	            raise RuntimeError(
115	                "orchestration requires the 'orchestration' extra (langgraph) — not installed"
116	            ) from exc
117	        proposal = dict(propose(state))
118	        current: dict[str, Any] = {"gate_id": gate.name, "proposal": proposal}
119	        # Validate inside a loop: on a bad/mis-correlated resume, re-fire interrupt (next
120	        # resume index) carrying the error rather than raising — a raise would strand the
121	        # bad value in the checkpoint and replay it forever, bricking the gate.
122	        while True:
123	            resumed = interrupt(current)
124	            try:
125	                update = apply_gate_decision(gate, resumed)
126	            except GateError as exc:
127	                current = {"gate_id": gate.name, "proposal": proposal, "error": str(exc)}
128	                continue
129	            if recorder is not None:
130	                for rec in update.get("gate_decisions", []):
131	                    recorder(rec)  # land the decision in the hash-chained provenance trail
132	            return update
133	
134	    return node
135

diff --git a/src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py b/src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py
index 88f3806..8f084ae 100644
--- a/src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py
+++ b/src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py
@@ -83,14 +83,26 @@ def already_decided(state: Mapping[str, Any], gate_name: str) -> bool:
     return False
 
 
+GateRecorder = Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], None]
+
+
 def make_gate_node(
-    gate: Gate, propose: GateProposal
+    gate: Gate,
+    propose: GateProposal,
+    *,
+    recorder: GateRecorder | None = None,
 ) -> Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], dict[str, Any]]:
     """Build a LangGraph node that parks at ``gate`` for an operator decision.
 
     ``propose(state)`` returns the proposal payload surfaced to the operator (what the gate is
     asking them to approve). The node returns a ``{"gate_decisions": [record]}`` update, or
     ``{}`` (no-op) if the gate is already terminally approved.
+
+    ``recorder`` (optional) bridges each validated decision into the hash-chained provenance
+    trail (#111): the durable LangGraph path otherwise records gate decisions only as
+    (untamper-evident) graph state, so the documented append-only trail would miss them. Pass a
+    callback such as ``lambda r: trail.record("gate", f"gate {r['gate']}: {r['decision']}", **r)``
+    so the graph path's approvals/rejections land in the same chained trail as the runner's.
     """
 
     def node(state: Mapping[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
@@ -110,8 +122,13 @@ def make_gate_node(
         while True:
             resumed = interrupt(current)
             try:
-                return apply_gate_decision(gate, resumed)
+                update = apply_gate_decision(gate, resumed)
             except GateError as exc:
                 current = {"gate_id": gate.name, "proposal": proposal, "error": str(exc)}
+                continue
+            if recorder is not None:
+                for rec in update.get("gate_decisions", []):
+                    recorder(rec)  # land the decision in the hash-chained provenance trail
+            return update
 
     return node

 from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping

         self._secret: bytes | None = bytes(secret) if secret is not None else None
         self._clock: Clock = clock or _utc_now

content.text: Test passes. Now let me verify the docs reconciliation (GOVERNANCE.md lines 47-50 that called the unchained list "the append-only trail"), and check the `entries` property and `verify_records` exist.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && git diff main -- GOVERNANCE.md docs/ 2>/dev/null | grep -iE "append-only|gate_decisions|chain|^\+|^-" | grep -iE "append-only|gate_decision|chain" | head -40

cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && grep -rn "append-only trail\|gate_decisions" GOVERNANCE.md docs/*.md 2>/dev/null | head -30; echo "---entries/verify---"; grep -n "def verify_records\|def entries\|entries" src/assay_engine/provenance.py | head
input.description: Grep docs for append-only trail claims and verify API

content.content:
+   reliability/error-handling/resource-leaks, performance/scale, ops/packaging/supply-chain,
+| 3 | HIGH | ops-packaging-supplychain | CI integration lane runs `pytest --cov` but pytest-cov is not a declared dependency — the lane fails on every run | `ci.yml` 73 |
+| 8 | MEDIUM | ops-packaging-supplychain | No supply-chain vulnerability scanning (pip-audit) in CI for a security/privacy-focused production engine | `ci.yml` 8 |
+| 11 | MEDIUM | observability-provenance | Graph-orchestration gate decisions never enter the hash-chained provenance trail — the documented “append-only trail” for the durable path is unchained, untamper-evident LangGraph state | `gatenode.py` 44-70 (apply_gate_decision returns {"gate_decisions": [record]}); 86-117 (make_gate_node) |

+### 8. [MEDIUM] No supply-chain vulnerability scanning (pip-audit) in CI for a security/privacy-focused production engine
+- **Problem:** Neither pyproject.toml nor ci.yml contains any dependency-vulnerability scanning step (`grep -n 'pip-audit\|safety\|bandit'` returns nothing). The project pulls a large, fast-moving transitive surface through its extras — verified by resolving the `observability` extra alone, which installs 150+ packages including cryptography, aiohttp, Flask/Werkzeug, requests, protobuf, grpcio, openai, anthropic, boto3, and SQLAlchemy. With only floor+major-cap ranges (e.g. `cryptography` unconstrained as a transitive, `aiohttp` transitive, `requests` transitive), a clean install on any future day can pull a transitive version with a freshly-disclosed CVE and CI will never notice. For a tool that brands itself for security & privacy data and is being hardened for production, the absence of an advisory-database check (and of a lockfile / constraints file for the heavy lanes) is a genuine supply-chain gap rather than a style preference.
+- **Verifier 1:** Confirmed as a genuine production-readiness ops/supply-chain gap, not a false positive or a justified design choice.
+- **Suggested fix:** Wrap the phase machine body in try/except/finally: on any exception, before re-raising, record a terminal entry e.g. trail.record('run_failed', f'{type(exc).__name__} during {visited[-1].name if visited else "start"}', error_type=type(exc).__name__, message=str(exc), last_phase=visited[-1].name if visited else None). Keep it inside the chain (so verify_records still passes) and guard the record() call itself so a provenance failure during teardown does not mask the original exception. This makes the partial trail self-describing: it ends with an explicit, chained 'this is why the run stopped' entry.
+This breaks a DOCUMENTED contract. GOVERNANCE.md §3 lines 100-103 and the run_study docstring (pipeline.py 203-204) explicitly promise that a caller may pass its own trail so a run that *raises* (a blocked gate, a firewall violation) still leaves an auditable partial trail. The trail survives and is chain-valid, but it is not self-describing on the failure path the feature exists to serve.
+The suggested fix is feasible and chain-safe: ProvenanceTrail.record() (provenance.py 112-150) appends an entry that extends the hash chain, so a terminal run_failed entry would keep verify()/verify_records() passing. 
+### 11. [MEDIUM] Graph-orchestration gate decisions never enter the hash-chained provenance trail — the documented “append-only trail” for the durable path is unchained, untamper-evident LangGraph state
+- **Location:** `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py` 44-70 (apply_gate_decision returns {"gate_decisions": [record]}); 86-117 (make_gate_node)
+- **Problem:** There are two governance mechanisms (ADR-0010): the synchronous run_study path records every gate decision into the hash-chained ProvenanceTrail (provenance.py), and the durable LangGraph interrupt/resume path (gatenode.make_gate_node) records decisions as plain dicts appended to a LangGraph state list 'gate_decisions'. GOVERNANCE §3 states the chained ProvenanceTrail "is the spine… No step can be retroactively removed or reordered," and GOVERNANCE.md lines 47-50 call the graph's gate_decisions list "the append-only trail." But the graph path's decisions are NEVER written into the chained ProvenanceTrail: apply_gate_decision takes no trail/recorder and returns a bare dict (PoC confirmed: {'gate_decisions': [{'gate':'review','decision':'rejected','rationale':'no good'}]}), and grep shows no bridge from gate_decisions into ProvenanceTrail (the as_recorder bridge at provenance.py:152 is wired only to the synchronous Gate.evaluate path, not the gatenode). The graph's 'append-only trail' has no prev_hash/entry_hash, no verify_records, and relies entirely on the study correctly declaring a list/add reducer (a documented foot-gun: lines 47-50 warn that a wrong reducer silently drops all but the last decision). So for the durable, cross-process governance path — the one production runs that park for an operator actually use — the most governance-relevant mutations (human approve/reject decisions) are recorded only as unchained, tamper-evident-free state that can be reordered or dropped without detection. The integrity guarantee GOVERNANCE §3 advertises does not hold for this path.
+- **Suggested fix:** Bridge graph gate decisions into the chained trail: have make_gate_node (or a thin wrapper) accept a ProvenanceTrail-backed recorder and record each validated decision via trail.record('gate', ...) at apply time, so the durable path's approvals/rejections land in the same hash-chained, verify_records-checkable trail as the synchronous path. At minimum, reconcile the docs to stop calling the unchained gate_decisions list an "append-only trail" with the same integrity as §3, and document that the graph path requires an explicit provenance bridge for a tamper-evident governance record.
+- **Verifier 1:** The factual core of the finding is verified against the real code. The durable LangGraph governance path (make_gate_node / apply_gate_decision in src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py) records operator approve/reject/defer decisions as plain dicts appended to LangGraph state under the key "gate_decisions" and NEVER writes them into the hash-chained ProvenanceTrail. I confirmed by reading gatenode.py (lines 44-70, 86-117): the function signatures take no trail/recorder (apply_gate_decision(gate, resumed); make_gate_node(gate, propose)), and grep shows the module never references evaluate/record/trail/recorder/precondition — the Gate object is used only for gate.name and validation. apply_gate_decision returns {"gate_decisions": [record]} (PoC reproduced exactly). The chained-trail bridge ProvenanceTrail.as_recorder (provenance.py:152) is wired only into the synchronous Gate.evaluate path (gates.py:97-98) and into run_study's run_gate -> trail.record("gate", ...) (pipeline.py:232-239); make_gate_node bypasses both.
+- **Verifier 2:** The finding's factual claims are accurate and independently confirmed. (1) The standalone LangGraph governance path does NOT chain gate decisions: apply_gate_decision(gate, resumed) returns a bare dict {"gate_decisions": [record]} with no prev_hash/entry_hash, and make_gate_node accepts no ProvenanceTrail/recorder. (2) There is no bridge from gate_decisions into ProvenanceTrail — grep over gatenode.py and graph.py shows zero references to trail/record/ProvenanceTrail/recorder. The chained-trail's as_recorder bridge (provenance.py:152) is wired only to the synchronous Gate.evaluate/run_study path. (3) The §3 ProvenanceTrail is the only mechanism providing prev_hash/entry_hash chaining and verify_records tamper-evidence; the graph path's decisions get none of that.\n\nThe documentation overstatement is real but narrow. GOVERNANCE.md line 50 refers to the LangGraph gate_decisions list as "the append-only trail" ("...the append-only trail would lose all but the last decision"), terminologically conflating it with the §3 hash-chained trail whose advertised guarantee is "no step can be retroactively removed or reordered" plus verifiability. For a study that builds its own LangGraph topol
+- **Problem:** record() computes `seq = len(self._entries)` and `prev_hash = self._entries[-1].entry_hash` (read), then does work (clock, digest, freeze), then `self._entries.append(entry)` (write), with no lock. If a single ProvenanceTrail were shared across threads, two concurrent record() calls could read the same seq/prev_hash and append two entries that both claim e.g. seq=N with the same prev_hash, breaking the chain so verify_records() (which asserts e.seq == i and e.prev_hash == prev) would fail — a corrupted append-only audit trail, the exact 'no step can be removed or reordered' guarantee the trail exists to provide.
+Technical claim verified by reading src/assay_engine/provenance.py:112-150. record() reads shared mutable state without a lock — seq=len(self._entries) (line 116) and prev_hash=self._entries[-1].entry_hash (line 117) — then does clock/normalize/_digest work (lines 118-134), then appends (line 149). The read-compute-append is a non-atomic check-then-act on self._entries. I reproduced the corruption with a PoC against the venv: by wedging _digest behind a 2-thread Barrier (widening the window between the seq/prev read and the append), two concurrent record() calls both read seq=0/prev_hash=genesis and append two entries both claiming seq=0, and verify_records() then raised 'provenance entry 1 has seq 0 (reordered or removed)'. So a shared trail under concurrency would silently corrupt the append-only chain — the exact 'no step removed or reordered' guarantee the trail exists to provide (GOVERNANCE §3, module docstring).
+- **Verifier 2:** The defect is real and I reproduced it: record() performs an unlocked read-compute-append (seq/prev_hash read at lines 116-117, append at 149). A forced-window PoC made 20 concurrent threads all read seq=0/genesis, produced 20 entries claiming seq=0, and verify() failed — exactly the corrupted append-only audit trail the prior assessor described. This is the core integrity guarantee of the class ('no step can be removed or reordered'), so a thread-unsafe write path is a genuine correctness defect in a public API method, not a style nit.
+- **Problem:** `pyproject.toml` sets `version = "0.0.1"` (line 7) while `src/assay_engine/__init__.py` separately hardcodes `__version__ = "0.0.1"` (line 28). These are two independent strings with no single source of truth — hatchling is not configured for dynamic versioning (`grep dynamic`/`hatch.version` returns nothing). This is not merely a style nit: `src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py` imports `__version__ as _ENGINE_VERSION` (line 24) and stamps it into the determinism/provenance record as `component_versions["engine"]` (line 111), which is part of the hash-chained reproducibility trail. A release that bumps only one of the two strings (the common failure mode) ships a wheel whose distributed version disagrees with the version recorded in every provenance manifest it produces — silently undermining the project's central reproducibility guarantee while passing all tests.
+4. That determinism record flows into the hash-chained provenance trail: pipeline.py:347 passes `determinism=dict(baseline.determinism)` into a provenance entry (built blind under Firewall A).
+So the dual-source-of-truth and the drift surface are real. This is a genuine production-readiness/supply-chain hygien

content.content:
30	
31	A gate node parks the run via an `interrupt` and resumes when the operator's decision is
32	delivered (`{gate_id, decision, rationale}`). The engine enforces three properties so the
33	governance trail is sound; a cloning study must respect the state-shape requirement:
34	
35	- **Correlation guard** — a resumed decision must name the gate currently parked; a stale or
36	  misrouted decision aimed at another gate is refused, not applied to whatever is waiting.
37	- **Recoverable, never bricked** — `interrupt` persists the resume value into the checkpoint
38	  before the node returns, so a malformed/mis-correlated value re-fires a fresh interrupt
39	  (the operator can re-submit) instead of raising and stranding the gate forever. Only an
40	  **approved** decision is terminal (re-entry is a no-op); a **rejected/deferred** gate
41	  re-prompts so a revise→re-review loop works.
42	- **Durable parking** — gate graphs must be compiled with the durable checkpointer
43	  (`compile_graph(..., requires_checkpointer=True)`); without one `interrupt` is a silent
44	  no-op and the gate would never pause. Resume must use the *same* checkpointer (keyed by
45	  `run_id` as `thread_id`).
46	
47	**State-shape requirement for cloners:** gate decisions are returned as
48	`{"gate_decisions": [record]}` partial updates, so a study's graph state must declare
49	`gate_decisions` with a list/append reducer — otherwise each gate would overwrite the prior
50	one and the append-only trail would lose all but the last decision.
51	
52	## 2. Pre-registration
53	
54	Before any confirmatory test runs, the relevant hypotheses are:
55	
56	1. made specific and typed (what is claimed, the test, the data, the decision rule),
57	2. content-hashed and committed,
58	3. **RFC-3161 timestamped** against a trusted timestamp authority.
59	
60	This is honestly characterized as a **timestamped adaptive design**: methods are fixed
61	first; the data informs the questions; the questions are then locked and timestamped before
62	they are tested. It is not claimed to be "predictions registered before any data."
63	
64	> **Implementation status (engine):** pre-registration is enforced by construction **in the
65	> confirmatory runners** (`adjudicate`, `discover_and_confirm`; ADR-0009). They verify, via a
66	> supplied `TimestampAuthority`, that a hypothesis's proof **binds its content and id** (the
67	> canonical digest of its decision-bearing fields), that the attested **timestamp is
68	> verifiable**, and that the lock **precedes confirmation** — a hand-set sentinel string, a
69	> post-lock content/id swap, a forged proof, or a lock dated at/after the ordering instant are

content.text:
Now I have the full picture. Let me assess.

The finding's suggested_fix had two parts:
1. **Primary**: Bridge graph gate decisions into the chained trail via a recorder. ✓ Done — `recorder` param added, regression test passes with `verify_records`.
2. **"At minimum"**: Reconcile the docs to stop calling the unchained `gate_decisions` list an "append-only trail" with §3 integrity.

The doc at GOVERNANCE.md:50 still says "the append-only trail would lose all but the last decision" — the exact phrasing the finding flagged as terminologically conflating the unchained list with the §3 hash-chained trail. This was NOT changed.

Let me assess the impact. The primary root-cause fix (the recorder bridge) is implemented well and the docstring of `make_gate_node` now documents that the bridge is optional and how to wire it. The doc overstatement was the "at minimum" fallback. The core mechanism is fixed. But is it *complete*?

Key consideration: the `recorder` is **optional** (`recorder: GateRecorder | None = None`). So a study that builds its own graph and forgets to pass a recorder still gets the unchained behavior — the foot-gun persists, just now opt-in to fix. That's an acceptable design (engine can't force studies). The docstring documents it. However, the doc that overstates integrity (line 50) remains unreconciled, and there's no doc update telling cloners they must pass a recorder for a tamper-evident governance record.

Let me verify the test would actually catch a regression (i.e., that recorder is called per decision).

input.description: Run full orchestration graph test file

content.text:
All tests pass. Let me confirm my assessment and provide the structured output.

The primary root-cause fix is sound and tested. The "at minimum" doc reconciliation portion of the suggested_fix was not done — GOVERNANCE.md:50 still calls the unchained `gate_decisions` list "the append-only trail," and no doc tells cloners they must pass a `recorder` for a tamper-evident governance record. The `make_gate_node` docstring does document the bridge, which partially covers this, but the GOVERNANCE.md overstatement that the finding explicitly cited remains.

input.reasoning:
APPROPRIATE: The fix addresses the root cause in the sound, recommended way. make_gate_node now accepts an optional `recorder: GateRecorder | None` (keyword-only) and, after a decision is validated, calls recorder(rec) for each record in the update before returning — landing the durable LangGraph path's approve/reject/defer decisions in the same hash-chained ProvenanceTrail as the synchronous run_study path. This is exactly the bridge the suggested_fix's primary recommendation called for. It is not a hack: it preserves all existing hardened behaviors (correlation guard, re-prompt-not-raise loop, terminal-approval idempotency — the early-return on already_decided correctly emits no record and thus calls no recorder), and the engine-vs-study separation is respected by making the recorder injectable rather than hardcoding a trail. The make_gate_node docstring documents the bridge and shows the canonical wiring (lambda r: trail.record("gate", ...)). I verified ProvenanceTrail.entries (property, line 191) and verify_records exist and the chain is intact after a bridged decision.

NOT COMPLETE on two narrow points: (1) The suggested_fix's explicit "at minimum" clause — reconcile the docs to stop calling the unchained gate_decisions list an "append-only trail" with §3 integrity, and document that the graph path requires an explicit provenance bridge — was NOT done. docs/GOVERNANCE.md:50 still reads "...the append-only trail would lose all but the last decision," the exact terminological conflation both upstream verifiers flagged; GOVERNANCE.md received zero changes (git diff main -- docs/GOVERNANCE.md is empty). The State-shape-requirement section (lines 47-50) still does not tell a cloner that, without passing a recorder, the durable path's governance decisions are unchained/untamper-evident. (2) The recorder is optional and defaults to None, so a study that builds its own graph and omits it silently reverts to the original unchained behavior — acceptable as a design (engine can't force studies), but it makes the doc note doubly necessary, and that note is absent. The regression test itself is good and WOULD catch a code regression of the bridge.</reasoning>

REGRESSION TEST: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/tests/test_orchestration_graph.py:110-126 (test_gate_node_bridges_decision_into_provenance_trail) — builds a real ProvenanceTrail, wires recorder=lambda r: trail.record("gate", ...), runs the node, then calls verify_records(trail.entries) and asserts the gate entry's decision == "approved". content.text: Verification complete for issue #111. Summary: the code fix is appropriate (sound recorder-bridge addressing the root cause, with a passing regression test using a real ProvenanceTrail + verify_records), but not complete because the documentation reconciliation explicitly required by the finding's "at minimum" clause was not done — docs/GOVERNANCE.md:50 still calls the unchained `gate_decisions` list "the append-only trail," and no doc tells cloners they must pass a `recorder` for a tamper-evident governance record.
